reasoning: The control AC-3(9) focuses on controlled release of information outside the system, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring that access is tightly controlled through RBAC and network policies. RBAC ensures that users and service accounts do not have excessive privileges, while network policies ensure that pods cannot communicate freely with external networks unless explicitly allowed. Additionally, restricting the use of LoadBalancer services can prevent unintended exposure of services to the internet.
